title: Registering at the Municipal Office
mission: Register your arrival and understand which documents and next steps are required.
learning_objectives: State the purpose of the visit (registration)
Give name, address, and arrival date understandably
Understand which documents are still needed

preparation_phrases.de: Ich habe einen Termin um … Uhr.
preparation_phrases.en: I have an appointment at … o'clock.

preparation_phrases.de: Ich habe meinen Pass und meinen Arbeitsvertrag.
preparation_phrases.en: I have my passport and my employment contract.
Which documents do I still need?
Could you please speak more slowly?

conversation_stages.name: greeting_and_appointment
conversation_stages.description: Clerk greets once with 'Grüezi', asks exactly one question: whether the learner has an appointment. Learner states purpose (anmelden) and confirms appointment.

conversation_stages.name: name
conversation_stages.description: Clerk asks for the learner's name, requests spelling only if unclear.

conversation_stages.name: address
conversation_stages.description: Clerk asks for the learner's address in Zurich. Temporary accommodation (hotel, sublet) is acceptable.

conversation_stages.name: arrival_and_reason
conversation_stages.description: Clerk asks when the learner arrived, then separately why they are in Switzerland. If work is mentioned, clerk may note registration must happen before the first working day.

conversation_stages.name: family_status
conversation_stages.description: Clerk asks about marital status, then separately about children.

conversation_stages.name: documents_and_insurance
conversation_stages.description: Clerk asks which documents the learner brought, confirms passport with visa and employment contract are sufficient, explains that a rental contract or landlord confirmation and one passport photo are still needed. Clerk asks about Swiss health insurance; if not yet arranged, explains the 3-month deadline.

conversation_stages.name: fee_and_next_steps
conversation_stages.description: Clerk states the fee is CHF 50. Explains simply: confirmation issued now, then a biometrics appointment at the cantonal migration office, then the permit card arrives by post.

conversation_stages.name: closing
conversation_stages.description: Clerk confirms registration is complete or states what is still needed, then closes politely. Ends the role-play and triggers summary generation.

success_criteria: Learner stated the purpose of the visit (registration)
Learner gave name, address, and arrival date understandably
Conversation reached what documents are still needed or what happens next
factual_constraints: Registration is required within 14 days of arrival
Registration must happen before the first working day
Health insurance must be arranged within 3 months of registration
Required documents: passport with visa, employment contract, rental contract or landlord confirmation, one passport photo
The fee at this office is CHF 50
After registration: biometrics appointment at the cantonal migration office, then the permit card (Aufenthaltsbewilligung) arrives by post

help_rules.repeat: Repeat the last sentence more slowly or with simpler words.
help_rules.english_help: If the learner says they don't understand, asks for English, or answers in English a second time: give the English translation of the current question once, then repeat the German question.
help_rules.speak_slower: If requested, use slower speech for the rest of the conversation.

result_mapping.0-1: Good try — let's run it again.
